metric_value: Context: follow-up to a prior-month meeting between Kastner, Mark Weber (Gibson Dunn), and John Casey (Intel) regarding the AMD v Intel antitrust case.
Enclosure 1: Internetweek column reporting a 2003 survey by Kastner of 250 companies on PC replacement buying plans (column no longer on the web at time of letter).
notes: Primary reference to Kastner's 2003 250-company PC survey.

metric_value: Enclosure 2: copy of Kastner's June 1999 expert witness report in Intelligent Electronics v Andersen Consulting, cited as demonstration of his analytical style for complex technology litigation.
notes: Cross-links to the IE v Andersen expert report (separately archived).

metric_value: Kastner's self-described analytical style: 'what is the impact of technology on business and how do business people deal with deploying technology for business advantage.'
Kastner, having read many of the public documents in the case, stated he 'can agree with Intel's publicly stated conclusion that it has an affirmative defense' in AMD v Intel.
notes: Relevant: Kastner's position prior to engagement.

metric_value: Kastner offered his services to Gibson Dunn's AMD v Intel team, stating he has 'the experience to add value.'
Scott-Page Associates address in 2007: 401 Pine Hill Rd., PO Box 3685, Westport MA 02790; cell (508) 264-6451.
